title: TEM processing for cytoskeleton and matix preservation in tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2dqe4","text":"Processing schedule  to allow good cytoskeleton and extracelluar matrix preservation and visibility in tissues. Ultrathin sections can be examined in TEM without post-staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ocl0","text":"All tissue pieces have to be cut to small size of about 1-3mm cubed to get enough staining. One of the dimensions should not be more than 1mm, others can be 2-3mm.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b2moi","text":"Tissue should be dissected as soon as possible after excision from an animal to avoid hypoxia artifacts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"92sll","text":"Wear protective gear (gloves, labcoat, goggles) when working with toxic materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Fix with 2.5% glutaraldehyde and 4% formaldehyde in 0.1M HEM buffer (pH 7.2) for at least 1hour.
Rinse with 0.1M HEM buffer (pH 7.2) 3x5min.
Incubate with freshly made mixture of 1% osmium tetroxide and1.5% potassium ferrocyanide in 0.1M cacodylate buffer (on rotator).
Rinse with distilled water 3x5min.
Incubate with 1% tannic acid (low molecular weight) in 0.1M cacodylate buffer (pH7.2) for 1h (onrotator).
Rinse with distilled water 3x5min.
Incubate in aqueous 1% uranyl acetate solution for at least2hr (better – overnight). *
Rinse with distilled water 3x5min.
70% ethanol in water (could be left overnight)
90% ethanol in water
100% ethanol

Prepare fresh resin formulation according to the manufacturer instructions. For better miscibility the resin components (except accelerator) can be warmed up in 60C oven. Mix all components very well by shaking the resin bottle. Leave the bottle with full resin mix in 60C oven for a while (about 10min) to let all bubbles (after shaking) to escape.
Incubate tissue pieces in 25% resin mix in acetone
100% ethanol
100% acetone
100% acetone
Incubate tissue pieces in 75% resin mix in acetone
Incubate tissue pieces in 100% resin mix
Put tissue pieces into resin- prefilled silicon moulds.
Log the mould numbers or put sample ID labels (laser printed on small pieces of paper, font 6pt) inside. Top up moulds with resin so the upper surface would be a bit convex.
Put moulds into 60C oven for overnight or longer (can be over a weekend)
Ultrathin sections can be examined in TEM without post-staining.
creator: Alexander Mironov
